column6: The supplemental insertion of this Headword should be placed just before the word "karkāru m. [ID=44745]" in the Hierarchical listing of words. This is simply according to the proper alphabetical placement, since their appears to be no other overriding factor.

column6: Please note that the Previous Headword Listing, is Precisely the SAME Spelling, and likely to be Directly Related: "kāpyakāraḥ A penitent.                  [ID=11984.002]". Therefore, Some Sort of "Merging" or "Consolidation" of Headwords and Meanings may be in Order? OTHERWISE, we are stuck with TWO Identical Headwords Being Separately Listed. If such is the case, then when a user "Searches" for this particular word, ONLY ONE Version would be shown from the initial Search; and then the user would have to Click Again on the Second Headword Listing, in order to discover All Meanings Available to this (currently) Dual Headword: "kāpyakāraḥ".

Please note that by Further Compounding, the Headword "kāmāgnisaṃdīpanam" is listed here by the abbreviation: "˚saṃdīpanam". Therefore this Additional Compound, should be listed as an Additional Headword in the Hierarchy Display Column, so as to be "Searchable" by the user. Of Course the related TWO definitions must accompany this Newly Listed Headword, which of course will receive an addition Indentation in the Hierarchy List Display beneath the pūrvapada: "kāmāgni".
